channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: La ansiedad no me deja dormir. ¿Cómo puedo conciliar el sueño?
item.description: Consultamos a cuatro expertos en estrés y sus efectos en el sueño sobre cómo controlar las preocupaciones que no te dejan dormir.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Schupak

media:content.media:credit: Eric Helgas para The New York Times

item.title: How’s He Doing? 13 New Yorkers Weigh In on Mamdani’s First 100 Days.
item.description: We talked to voters in neighborhoods where Zohran Mamdani is revered, in areas where he is feared and in diverse parts of the city where opinions differ.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos

media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s 100th day in office will be on Friday.

item.title: Making Friends
item.description: What is your best advice when it comes to friendship?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 8, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Poll Shows Optimism in New York’s Future With Mamdani as Mayor
item.description: A new Marist poll found that about half of New Yorkers approved of Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s job performance, while a larger share of respondents believed the city was moving in the right direction.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos

media:content.media:credit: Adam Gray for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The survey, conducted by the Marist Institute for Public Opinion, is the first high-quality, nonpartisan poll to capture New Yorkers’ views of Mayor Zohran Mamdani since he took office on Jan. 1.

item.title: Why You Can’t ‘Flick a Switch’ to Get Oil and Gas Flowing Out of the Persian Gulf
item.description: Some wells can be turned on in days or weeks, but bringing the Gulf’s energy system back to something akin to normal will take months.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott and Ivan Penn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Defendant in Gilgo Beach Murders May Plead Guilty on Wednesday
item.description: Rex Heuermann, who is charged with murdering seven women dating back to the 1990s, was arrested in 2023 and has pleaded not guilty. His trial is set to start after Labor Day.
item.dc:creator: Corey Kilgannon

media:content.media:description: If Rex Heuermann pleads guilty, he will most likely be in prison for the rest of his life.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: 2-Week Cease-Fire Takes Hold
item.description: Israel said the cease-fire did not include Lebanon. It was unclear whether ship operators considered the Strait of Hormuz safe for transit, or if word of the deal had reached local Iranian commanders.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Late Night Puzzles Over Trump’s ‘Mixed Message’ on Iran
item.description: “Everyone, most notably the people of Iran, were wondering if their civilization was going to die tonight. Well, good news, it didn’t,” Jimmy Kimmel said after a cease-fire was announced.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘expiate’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 2 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: expiate
item.description: This word has appeared in two articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: ¿Qué son las carreras Hyrox?
item.description: La carrera ha experimentado un crecimiento masivo en los últimos años. ¿Qué está impulsando su popularidad?
item.dc:creator: Danielle Friedman

media:content.media:credit: Mustafa Hussain para The New York Times

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: Tk
item.description: TK

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: Unraveling the Mystery Behind Bitcoin’s Creator
item.description: Our investigative reporter John Carreyrou spent 18 months digging through the archives of online cryptography communities in search of the identity of Satoshi Nakamoto, the anonymous inventor of bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: John Carreyrou, Sutton Raphael, James Surdam, Coleman Lowndes and Joey Sendaydiego

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: Oil Prices Plunge and Stocks Surge After Cease-Fire Deal
item.description: Markets reacted positively after the United States and Iran reached a last-minute cease-fire agreement.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Imágenes de la hija de Kim Jong-un en un tanque avivan debates de sucesión
item.description: El video y las fotografías de Kim Ju-ae al timón del vehículo militar, con su padre a bordo sobre la coraza, alimentaron las especulaciones de que está siendo preparada para sucederlo como líder de Corea del Norte.
item.dc:creator: Max Kim

media:content.media:credit: Korean Central News Agency/Korea News Service, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A photograph released by North Korea’s state media appeared to show the country’s leader, Kim Jong-un, and his daughter, Kim Ju-ae, riding a tank last month.

item.title: Corrections: April 8, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Wednesday, April 8, 2026.

item.title: Who Is Satoshi Nakamoto? My Quest to Unmask Bitcoin’s Creator
item.description: Bitcoin’s creator has hidden behind the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto for 17 years. But a trail of clues buried deep in crypto lore led to a 55-year-old computer scientist named Adam Back.
item.dc:creator: John Carreyrou and Dylan Freedman

item.title: 4 Takeaways From Our Search for Satoshi Nakamoto, Bitcoin’s Creator
item.description: Here’s what we found suggesting that Adam Back invented Bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: John Carreyrou and Dylan Freedman

media:content.media:credit: Amir Hamja for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Adam Back in Miami in February.

item.title: How China Built Its Vast Natural Gas Stockpile
item.description: Natural gas is hard to store, but China has found a way to do it, while also developing alternate suppliers and expanding production at home.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: China’s natural gas stockpiles — the world’s largest above ground — helped the country cushion the shock from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: He Got Rich Buying and Selling Luxury Watches. Was It a Ponzi Scheme?
item.description: Dominic Khoo made waves in Singapore as an investor in expensive timepieces. Now many of his clients accuse him of fraud.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee

media:content.media:credit: Ore Huiying for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Luxury watches bought from a business called the WatchFund.

item.title: Quote of the Day: LATE DELIBERATION AS TRUMP ISSUES OMINOUS THREATS
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, April 8, 2026.

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: TK
item.description: Tk

item.title: Tk
item.description: Tk

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: Democrat Wins Mayoral Race in Republican-Leaning Waukesha, Wis.
item.description: Alicia Halvensleben, the president of the city’s Common Council, defeated a Republican legislator, continuing a string of Trump-era mayoral wins for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith

media:content.media:description: Waukesha, Wis., is a city of about 70,000 people about 20 miles west of Milwaukee.

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: Lawmakers Greet Iran Cease-fire With Relief and More Questions
item.description: Democrats continued to raise serious questions about a path forward while Republican leaders were mostly mum on President Trump’s decision to de-escalate tensions.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lawmakers of both parties were relieved at the announcement of a cease-fire though Democrats had grave questions about the path forward.

item.title: In Georgia’s 14th District Special Runoff, Democrats Pulled Off Their Biggest Shift Yet
item.description: Shawn Harris lost by about 12 percentage points in the 14th Congressional District, but he shifted the district 25 points to the left.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler and Christine Zhang

item.title: Trump debilitó la posición de EE. UU. al amenazar a la civilización de Irán
item.description: Los últimos comentarios del mandatario llevan la marca de un líder acostumbrado a salirse con la suya y que ahora no está consiguiendo lo que quiere.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times

item.title: Hours Before News of Cease-Fire, Pope Leo XIV Issues Strong Rebuke of Trump
item.description: The first U.S.-born pontiff has consistently called for dialogue to resolve the war in the Middle East. He referred to Trump’s Iran threats as ‘truly unacceptable.’
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV condemned President Trump’s threats against Iran on Tuesday.

item.title: Gracie Mansion Suspects Wanted to Kill Up to 60 People, U.S. Says
item.description: An indictment charged two men with conspiracy to use a weapon of mass destruction in the homemade bomb attack near Mayor Zohran Mamdani's residence. It cited dashcam audio in which one said, “I want to start terror bro.”
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar
item.category: Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for April 8, 2026
item.description: Philippe Monfiston makes an elegant debut.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Aronow

media:content.media:credit: Julie Goldstone for The New York Times

item.title: Chris Taylor, a Liberal Judge, Wins Race for Wisconsin Supreme Court
item.description: With Judge Chris Taylor’s win, liberals increased their hold on the court. Races for the Wisconsin Supreme Court often draw national attention, but not this year.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman

media:content.media:description: Chris Taylor, a state appeals judge, was elected to the Wisconsin Supreme Court on Tuesday.

item.title: Inside the Race to Find a Downed U.S. Airman in Iran
item.description: Our reporter Helene Cooper walks us through the high-stakes operation to rescue a downed U.S. airman in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper, Gilad Thaler, Thomas Vollkommer, Laura Salaberry, Nikolay Nikolov, Nour Idriss, Ray Whitehouse and Paul Abowd

item.title: ‘D.E.I.’ Was Erased From N.Y.C. Racial Equity Plan to Avoid Conflict With Trump
item.description: The changes came as President Trump has moved to erase diversity, equity and inclusion efforts. A Justice Department official still said the report seemed “fishy/illegal.”
item.dc:creator: Jeffery C. Mays

media:content.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani released the racial equity plan along with a report on the true cost of living, as part of an effort to more closely link his affordability agenda to racial equity.

item.title: $35 Million or Your Money Back: One Candidate’s Silicon Valley Lifeline
item.description: Tech leaders see Matt Mahan, a moderate Democrat, as their best option in the California governor’s race, but tensions have risen as he has struggled to gain traction in polls.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer

media:content.media:description: Matt Mahan has entered the California governor’s race as a Silicon Valley ally opposed to a billionaire’s tax.

item.title: Traveling Without Sight: How Blind and Visually Impaired Explorers Navigate the World
item.description: A writer joined a 10-day journey through northern India — on a tour designed for the visually impaired. Here’s what he learned.

item.title: Anthropic’s Restraint Is a Terrifying Warning Sign
item.description: The rapid advance of artificial intelligence is happening now.
item.dc:creator: Thomas L. Friedman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Fanciful and Fabulous: ‘Cats: The Jellicle Ball’ on Broadway
item.description: For their 10th life, the cats strut and duckwalk in a reappraisal of Andrew Lloyd Webber’s 1981 musical, which has shifted to the queer ballroom scene.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw and Sara Krulwich

item.title: Clay Fuller, Trump Ally, Will Take Marjorie Taylor Greene’s Seat in Georgia
item.description: Democrats had hoped for a strong showing, but the conservative district in Northwest Georgia elected Clay Fuller after President Trump endorsed him.
item.dc:creator: Richard Fausset and Rick Rojas

media:content.media:description: Clay Fuller’s victory is a win for President Trump, who visited the district in February and appeared onstage with him.

item.title: Artemis II Astronauts Get a Break After Journey Around the Moon
item.description: The crew of the NASA mission had a quiet day as they flew home toward Earth.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang

media:content.media:description: The Artemis II astronauts aboard their Orion spacecraft, left, observed the moon eclipsing the sun on Monday for about 53 minutes.

item.title: Polls in Wisconsin Reflect a More Muted Supreme Court Race
item.description: This year’s election has featured more undecided voters and lower overall awareness compared with recent cycles.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler

media:content.media:credit: Joe Timmerman/Wisconsin Watch, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Ballot booths at Warner Park Community Recreation Center on in Madison on Tuesday.

item.title: Taylor Frankie Paul Files Competing Court Petition Against Dakota Mortensen
item.description: The reality star and her former boyfriend, Dakota Mortensen, have filed dueling court petitions that paint each other as the main aggressor in a recent altercation.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs
item.category: The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives (TV Program)

item.title: Acting Attorney General Says Only Trump Knows Why Bondi Was Fired
item.description: The acting attorney general, Todd Blanche, said during his first news conference on Tuesday that “nobody has any idea” why President Trump fired Pam Bondi last week.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

item.title: Republicans Keep Marjorie Taylor Greene’s Seat, but Democrats Narrow the Gap
item.description: Democrats significantly outperformed their 2024 result in the deep-red House district, adding to a string of strong showings. In Wisconsin, Chris Taylor, a liberal judge, won a seat on the state’s Supreme Court.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
